merger_name: Warburg Pincus – Commercial Credit Holdings Limited

anzsic_codes.name: Credit Reporting and Debt Collection Services

merger_description:
Currawong BidCo Pty Ltd (**BidCo**), a special purpose vehicle owned and controlled by funds managed by Warburg Pincus LLC (**Warburg Pincus**), proposes to acquire Commercial Credit Holdings Limited (**CCH**).

Warburg Pincus is a private equity firm headquartered in New York, USA. Warburg Pincus has more than US$100 billion in assets under management, and more than 215 companies in its active portfolio, diversified across stages, sectors, and geographies. In Australia, Warburg Pincus’ portfolio companies are primarily active in the software, financial services and real estate sectors.

CCH, through its subsidiary, Creditor Watch Pty Ltd, operates the CreditorWatch business, a commercial credit reporting bureau and end-to-end credit risk management platform serving Australian businesses. CreditorWatch has three core product offerings: (i) Onboard, which provides automated customer onboarding and identity verification; (ii) Manage, which provides commercial credit reports and customer risk monitoring; and (iii) Collect, which provides a portal for prioritising and managing the collection of invoices.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Currawong BidCo Pty Ltd, a special purpose vehicle owned and
controlled by funds managed by Warburg Pincus LLC (Warburg
Pincus), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
acquisition of 100% of the issued share capital in Commercial Credit
Holdings Limited (CCH), as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Warburg Pincus, is a private equity firm headquartered
in New York, USA. Warburg Pincus has more than US$100 billion in
assets under management, and more than 215 companies in its
active portfolio, diversified across stages, sectors, and geographies.
In Australia, Warburg Pincus’ portfolio companies are primarily active
in the software, financial services and real estate sectors.
The target, CCH, through its subsidiary, Creditor Watch Pty Ltd,
operates the CreditorWatch business (CreditorWatch), a commercial
credit reporting bureau and end-to-end credit risk management
platform serving Australian businesses. CreditorWatch has three
core product offerings: (i) Onboard, which provides automated
customer onboarding and identity verification; (ii) Manage, which
provides commercial credit reports and customer risk monitoring;
and (iii) Collect, which provides a portal for prioritising and managing
the collection of invoices.
Some Warburg Pincus controlled portfolio companies procure credit
reporting services in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between Warburg Pincus and
CCH (including their connected entities) in the supply of
credit reporting and ancillary services in Australia
b. there is a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical
effects resulting from exclusionary conduct post-acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
